evidence: Package structure: api/ (6 files), config/ (4 files), exploration/ (2 files), indexer/blacklab/ (2 files), model/ (9 files), query/ (4 files), utils/ (3 files), viz/ (1 file), root Main.java

model/ contains 9 files: AdjectiveProfile, ComparisonResult, CoreCollocate, DiscoveredNoun, Edge, ExplorationResult, ExploreOptions, QueryResults, RelationEdgeType — borderline flat overload but all are genuinely model classes

QueryResults.java in model/ acts as a namespace container for inner records WordSketchResult and CollocateResult — this is an organizational choice that reduces file count but obscures discoverability

PatternSubstitution.java lives in api/ but implements logic that mirrors RelationConfig.mergeLemmaConstraint (in config/) — same algorithm for merging lemma constraints into CQL tokens exists in two packages

RelationUtils.java in api/ is a static alias table (8 entries) that maps API-facing short names to grammar-config IDs; it has no HTTP-specific logic yet lives in api/ rather than config/ or a standalone aliases/ package
LongIntHashMap in utils/ is used only by the indexer (not visible in any query or api import); its placement in utils/ is slightly misaligned with its only consumer (indexer/blacklab/)
Main.java is the single root-level file; it has high fan-in (it is the entry point) so it is correctly placed at root
viz/ package has only one file (RadialPlot.java), making it a thin-wrapper package with no growth visible

strengths: Core separation of concerns is clear: api/, query/, exploration/, config/, model/ each have distinct responsibilities
exploration/ was extracted from query/ at some point — the two-file package is small but reflects a real semantic boundary
config/ is cohesive: RelationConfig, GrammarConfigLoader, RelationType, PosGroup all belong together
indexer/blacklab/ uses a subdirectory for the BlackLab implementation, anticipating alternative indexers
package_organization.issue_character: The main organizational issues are boundary violations where logic that serves config concerns (alias resolution, pattern substitution) is placed in the api/ package, and a thin viz/ package that does not justify its own directory.
package_organization.score_rationale: The package layout is mostly navigable and coherent, landing it in the 'adequate but improvable' range. The main issues — RelationUtils and PatternSubstitution in api/ having config-domain concerns, QueryResults bundling two distinct result types under a namespace class, and LongIntHashMap being in utils/ while only used by indexer/ — are friction points that increase cognitive overhead when navigating the codebase but do not cause coupling failures.

issues.summary: RelationUtils (alias table) placed in api/ despite having no HTTP-specific logic
evidence: RelationUtils.RELATION_ALIASES maps short user-facing names (adj_modifier, adj_predicate, subject_of, object_of) to canonical grammar-config IDs
This alias table is semantically part of the grammar config contract, not the HTTP layer
The Javadoc itself says 'If new relations are added to the grammar config, register their aliases here' — the coupling is to config/, not api/
issues.suggestion: Move RelationUtils to config/ package as GrammarAliases or merge it into GrammarConfigLoader as a resolveAlias(String) method. Update imports in ExplorationHandlers. Staged plan: (1) move file, (2) update package declaration, (3) update import in ExplorationHandlers.java, (4) run mvn test to verify.

issues.summary: PatternSubstitution in api/ duplicates CQL constraint-merging logic already in RelationConfig (config/)
evidence: PatternSubstitution.substituteCollocate builds a new [lemma=... & xpos=...] constraint by calling CqlUtils.extractConstraintAttribute — identical approach to RelationConfig.mergeLemmaConstraint
RelationConfig.getFullPattern(String, String) performs the same two-step substitution (head then collocate) that PatternSubstitution performs manually in SketchHandlers.handleConcordanceExamples
issues.suggestion: Remove PatternSubstitution.java and have SketchHandlers call rel.getFullPattern(word1, word2) directly, which already handles both substitutions. Validate by running PatternSubstitutionTest (it can be removed once migrated) and HandlersTest.

issues.summary: QueryResults.java bundles unrelated result records under a single namespace class, obscuring discoverability
evidence: QueryResults.java contains inner records: WordSketchResult, CollocateResult, ConcordanceResult, SnippetResult
WordSketchResult is used in exploration and sketch contexts; CollocateResult is used in concordance/BCQL contexts — they have different consumers
Navigating to WordSketchResult requires knowing it is inside QueryResults.java
issues.suggestion: Extract each inner record into its own top-level file in model/: WordSketchResult.java, CollocateResult.java, SnippetResult.java. Staged plan: (1) create new files, (2) update all import sites, (3) delete QueryResults.java, (4) run mvn test.

root_causes: api/ package accumulated config-adjacent utilities over time without placement review
QueryResults namespace pattern likely originated as a convenience that was never revisited
likely_symptoms: pattern_substitution_duplicated_across_packages is the most actionable — it is a real duplication not just organizational preference
possible_false_positives: viz/ being a thin package is low-impact since RadialPlot is a cohesive standalone component
